title: An update from a 2019 letter writer
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: A 2019 letter writer updated that showing their letter to a partner helped save their relationship.
The couple, initially struggling, pursued individual therapy and later decided to marry, emphasizing growth and joy.
Both partners embraced personal adventures before marriage, leading to a fulfilling relationship and happy ending.

title: Officials: Man who shot 2 in Hampton Beach before dying by suicide was active-duty Navy sailor
author: Audrey Cooney
bullets: Tyshawn Cooper, an active-duty Navy sailor, shot two people in Hampton Beach, then fatally shot himself, officials said.
Cooper, 28, assigned to the USS Hampton, resided in New Hampshire due to his Navy assignment, authorities confirmed.
New Hampshire State Police and Naval Criminal Investigative Service are jointly investigating the shooting incident.

title: Report: Celtics’ Paul George turned down $3.9 million trade bonus
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Paul George waived his $3.9 million trade bonus, keeping his Celtics cap hit at $54.1 million.
The Celtics are $30 million over the salary cap, with $25 million space before hitting the luxury tax apron.
The second apron penalties include restrictions on trades and draft picks, impacting team strategy and finances.

title: R.I. food bank thanks Taylor Swift, Travis Kelce for pre-wedding donation
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce donated $1 million to the Rhode Island Community Food Bank before their New York City wedding.
The donation will help purchase and distribute nutritious food to 137 member agencies served by the food bank.
The couple also contributed to other food banks, including $2 million to Feeding America and $1 million to Harvesters.

title: 5 takeaways from the Celtics’ press conference about the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George and draft assets, citing financial constraints under the new CBA.
Brad Stevens emphasized the need for 'optionality' in trades, balancing immediate competitiveness and future assets.
There is concern over Celtics' treatment of Brown, with Bill Chisholm stating the need to maintain a positive team reputation.

title: 5 takeaways from the U.S. loss to Belgium in the World Cup Round of 16
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The United States was defeated by Belgium 4-1 in the World Cup Round of 16 in Seattle.
Belgian player Charles De Ketelaere scored two goals, while a mistake by U.S. goalkeeper led to another goal.
Belgium's lineup changes outperformed the predictable U.S. formation, which struggled with defensive errors and ineffective attacks.

title: 2 siblings drown in Vermont river, brother tried to save younger sister, police say
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Sandro Lala, 25, and Yazmin Yupangui, 11, drowned while in the Lamoille River in Georgia, Vermont, last Wednesday.
Lala attempted to save his sister after she fell into the river. Their bodies were found Thursday and Friday.
A GoFundMe raised $70,000 to cover funeral expenses and transport the siblings to Ecuador for burial.

title: City declines to use reserve funds to prevent BPS layoffs as more than 560 positions are cut
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Boston's Wu administration rejected using reserve funds to prevent over 560 Boston Public Schools job cuts.
The $1.7 billion BPS budget approved last month contributes to more than 400 layoffs amid enrollment declines.
Councilor Ed Flynn urged reassigning staff, citing layoffs' impact on low-income communities and vulnerable students.

title: Harmful bacteria persist in Merrimack River days after Haverhill sewer break
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Elevated bacteria levels persist in the Merrimack River over a week after sewage spill in Haverhill.
Plum Island beaches remain closed; shellfish harvesting halted in several North Shore areas due to contamination.
Emergency bypass installed; water and shellfish quality testing continues to meet national standards.

title: Missing boater found dead after crash off of Salisbury Beach
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: U.S. Coast Guard found the body of missing boater near Salisbury days after a crash.
The boater, identified as Vilmar Dorner, went missing following a crash into a jetty at Salisbury Beach.
Massachusetts State Police are investigating the incident, with the body now at the Chief Medical Examiner's office.
